All done. Here's the summary:

### Escalation Sweep Results

**Hot services: 4**

| Service | Unresolved Sentry Issues |
|---|---|
| web-frontend | 8 |
| billing | 7 |
| api-gateway | 7 |
| mobile-bff | 7 |

**PagerDuty incidents acknowledged: 5**
- **PD-407** — mobile-bff: Checkout latency spikes at peak (high)
- **PD-416** — billing: SSO SCIM provisioning errors (high)
- **PD-421** — mobile-bff: Image uploads time out over 5MB (low)
- **PD-424** — api-gateway: Billing webhook retries drop events (low)
- **PD-433** — billing: Image uploads time out over 5MB (high)

**Email sent** to `oncall@acme.io` with subject "Escalation" listing all hot services and their issue counts.
I'll start by discovering the relevant table schemas, then run the escalation sweep.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'sentry_issues' ORDER BY ordinal_position"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'pagerduty_incidents' ORDER BY ordinal_position"}

text: Good. Now let me identify hot services.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT project, COUNT(*) AS unresolved_count
FROM sentry_issues
WHERE status = 'unresolved'
GROUP BY project
HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
ORDER BY unresolved_count DESC"}

text: Found **4 hot services**. Now I'll acknowledge the triggered PagerDuty incidents for those services and send the escalation email.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"UPDATE pagerduty_incidents
SET status = 'acknowledged'
WHERE status = 'triggered'
  AND service IN ('web-frontend', 'billing', 'api-gateway', 'mobile-bff')"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO emails (to_addr, subject, body)
SELECT
  'oncall@acme.io',
  'Escalation',
  'Hot services (more than 5 unresolved Sentry issues):' || E'\n\n' ||
  STRING_AGG(project || ': ' || cnt || ' unresolved issues', E'\n' ORDER BY cnt DESC)
FROM (
  SELECT project, COUNT(*)::text AS cnt
  FROM sentry_issues
  WHERE status = 'unresolved'
  GROUP BY project
  HAVING COUNT(*)…(+13)

message: MemoryBackend: unexpected token "\n\n" after a complete statement (did an earlier clause end early, or is this an unsupported feature?)

text: Let me fix both issues. I need to find the specific incident IDs and fix the email formatting.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, service, title, urgency FROM pagerduty_incidents WHERE status = 'triggered' AND service IN ('web-frontend', 'billing', 'api-gateway', 'mobile-bff')"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT project, COUNT(*) AS cnt
FROM sentry_issues
WHERE status = 'unresolved'
GROUP BY project
HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
ORDER BY cnt DESC"}

text: Good. Now I'll acknowledge all 5 triggered incidents and send the email in one transaction.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
